pages.raw_text:
Men, Money and Military Rule"
is the subject of the lecture to be
given this evening for the benefit Of
the striking miners of Colorado by
F. c.. Robertson, the attorney. The
sale of tickets continues today and a
large attendance is promised. Orches-
ira music and singing have been pro-
vided to add interest to the program:
Mr.. Robertson has written poem on
the subject which the Elks' glee club
win sing if the music can be arranged
in time.


The Trades council last night took
up the matter and appointed conf
mittees to assist. The stage committee
is William Quinn and Charles A. Foster
The treasurer is William Horn; the
auditing committee is Joseph F. Gutter
and ]. Hartwick; the door committee
is George Stone, H. Black, 9. F. Pal
mer, j. L. Miller and w. w. Lea.
